source_paper_title: Invariant States of Hydrodynamic Limits of Randomized Load-Balancing Networks
context:
Section 1.1 (Introduction), page 3; also reiterated as a conjecture in Section 4.3, page 11

Since the N-server stationary distributions are known to be tight (due to the results in [5]), to provide a rigorous proof of this convergence it would suffice to show that the solution to the hydrodynamic equations converges to the invariant state characterized here for a large class of initial conditions. Whereas this is a non-trivial problem that is relegated to future work, it is encouraging that analogous results have been recently established for a related problem.
title: Global convergence of the hydrodynamic equations to the physical invariant state (and interchange of limits with stationarity)
background_latex:
Consider the SQ\((d)\) load balancing model with \(N\) parallel FCFS servers. Jobs arrive at total rate \(\lambda N\) with \(\lambda\in(0,1)\), each job samples \(d\ge 2\) servers uniformly at random and joins the shortest sampled queue (ties broken uniformly). Service times are i.i.d. with distribution function \(G\) on \([0,\infty)\), density \(g\), and mean \(1\). Let \( ar G(x)=1-G(x)\) and define \(L:=\sup\{x\ge 0: ar G(x)>0\}\in(0,\infty]\). The mean-field (hydrodynamic) state is \(
u(t)=(
u_\ell(t))_{\ell\ge 1}\in S\), where for each \(\ell\ge 1\), \(
u_\ell(t)\) is a finite measure on \([0,L)\) representing the age distribution of jobs in service at queues of length at least \(\ell\). The hydrodynamic equations (Definition 2.1 in the paper) define a deterministic semiflow \(t\mapsto 
u(t)\) given an initial condition \(
u(0)\in S\). An invariant state is \(
u^*\in S\) such that the constant trajectory \(
u(t)\equiv 
u^*\) satisfies the hydrodynamic equations. A physical invariant state additionally satisfies \(s^*_\ell:=\langle 1,
u^*_\ellangle	o 0\) as \(\ell	o\infty\). For each \(N\), let \(\pi_N\) denote the stationary distribution of the \(N\)-server Markov state (in the same measure-valued representation, scaled by \(1/N\)), whose existence/uniqueness holds under subcriticality by known results cited in the paper.
problem_definition_latex:
Prove global convergence to equilibrium for the hydrodynamic equations, i.e., identify a broad class \(\mathcal I\subset S\) of initial conditions such that for every \(
u(0)\in\mathcal I\), the unique solution \(
u(t)\) of the hydrodynamic equations satisfies \(
u(t)	o 
u^*\) as \(t	o\infty\), where \(
u^*\) is the (physical) invariant state characterized in the paper. As a consequence, establish the steady-state limit/interchange: if the \(N\)-server systems are started in stationarity with law \(\pi_N\), then \(\pi_N\) converges as \(N	o\infty\) to the Dirac mass at \(
u^*\) (equivalently, the stationary queue-length tail probabilities converge to \(s^*=(s^*_\ell)_{\ell\ge 1}\)).
